title: Bits In Bio Weekly - July 23rd, 2024
text: 📢 Highlights Another week, another bio foundation model: meet NOETIK's OCTO The first mouse model with a complete human immune system has been bred Who said the market is cooling? We saw >$650M in funding in the last 8 days Not yet a member of our super awesome Slack community of >7000? Join HERE 🤗 👀 In Case You Missed it .. NOETIK's OCTO Model Looks to Revolutionize Cancer Biology San Francisco-based drug developer NOETIK has introduced the Oncology Counterfactual Therapeutics Oracle (OCTO) for next-generation cancer therapy development. OCTO is a massively multimodal transformer foundation model  pre-trained on large-scale human tumor data and fine-tuned using high-throughput in vivo experiments to learn a holistic biological representation. The founders Ron Alfa and Jacob Rinaldi previously led R&D at Recursion and have built a potent team with notable members including Lacey Padron from Parker Institute and Shaf Virani from Recursion. Since closing their $ 14M seed investment with DCVC last year, they’ve generated a massive dataset, trained their first large-scale “world models” and have initiated an early pipeline of therapeutics projects. Their AI-first approach aims to address oncological drug failures by targeting the correct biological markers, misses in which are associated both with negative off target effects and low efficacy. First Mouse Model with Human Immune System Developed In a development that may truly prove momentous in the context of drug development, scientists have successfully created the first mouse model with a complete, functional human immune system and a human-like gut microbiome. There is the joke that ML advances one dataset at a time, for bio it's one animal model at a time. This model can mount specific antibody responses, providing a valuable tool for studying human immune responses in cancer, autoimmune, and infectious diseases. There is good reason to be excited, with a rich body of literature ( example 1 and example 2 ) showing the positive  impact of the quality of models on drug discovery. Exscientia Acquires Full Rights to CDK7 Inhibitor Ahead of Phase 1 dose escalation data readout, Exscientia has acquired the full rights to the CDK7 inhibitor GTAEXS617 from GT Apeiron . The ELUCIDATE trial will evaluate '617 as a monotherapy and in combination with standard therapies for HR+/HER2- breast cancer. This acquisition, involving an upfront payment of $10 million in cash, $10 million in equity, strengthens Exscientia's oncology pipeline. The terms of the deal also include single-digit royalties on future sales. As Exscientia now holds the reins of what appears to be a promising program, questions arise about GT Apeiron’s decision to let go of its most advanced asse t.This could be a strategy from them to crystallize the value from this asset ahead of costly and unpredictable clinical trial outcomes, with some eyeing an upfront for this kind of asset being around $40M. LAB-Bench Proposes New AI Benchmark for Biology Research A recent paper titled LAB-Bench proposes approximately 2,500 multiple-choice questions to evaluate the capabilities of language models in practical biology research. This benchmark, coming from the dedicated team at the Future House non profit organization, covers tasks such as reasoning over literature, interpreting figures and tables, and manipulating DNA and protein sequences. Achieving high performance on LAB-Bench would indicate advancements in the practical applications of AI for scientific research, an area that is currently contentious but increasingly showing merit when applied to select use cases. CytoReason Secures $80 Million for AI Disease Models Israeli startup CytoReason has secured $80 million in series B round led by OurCrown with notable participation from  NVIDIA and Pfizer, to enhance its AI disease models. An earlier pioneer of knowledge graph-based discovery systems, CytoReason's graph-based methods side step several issues that plague RNN-based systems, such as hallucinations and lack of explainability. The funds will be used to expand its molecular and clinical database and explore new disease areas and markets. Briefly Bio Aims to Revolutionize Scientific Collaboration A British startup named Briefly Bio has launched a new tool designed to enhance collaboration among scientists by facilitating the sharing of research methods. Leaving stealth with a pre-seed raise of $1.2M , their platform helps researchers to share brief, easily digestible summaries of their lab work, making it more accessible and fostering a more collaborative scientific community. With the goal of accelerating scientific discovery with reproducible protocols, Briefly Bio has a fresh in a space hosting such 500lb (ahem, 226.796 kg) gorillas as Benchling and Code Ocean with offerings that have nonetheless failed to penetrate the industry fully. Blocking IL-11 Protein Extends Lifespan in Mice Researchers at Duke–NUS Medical School 'accidentally' discovered that blocking the IL-11 protein in mice extends their lifespan by 25%. This breakthrough improves metabolism and reduces frailty, suggesting potential anti-aging therapies for humans. The findings could lead to new approaches in age-related disease treatment, though will require ample follow on research to understand the nature of the biological mechanisms at play. 310 AI Launches MPM4 for Protein Sequence Design Introducing MPM4, 310 AI unveiled a generative text-to-protein sequence model that designs novel proteins based on specified functions. The model integrates with validation tools for structure modeling and function prediction, offering a repository of over 1000 high-quality AI-generated protein sequences. MPM4 advances the "Programming Problem" by enabling precise protein design with minimal prior experience or domain expertise in computational protein modeling and design, lowering the barrier to entry while optimizing hit-to-lead development. Integra Therapeutics Secures €10.5M for FiCAT Platform Barcelona-based Integra Therapeutics secured €10.5 million from the European Commission to advance its FiCAT gene writing platform. The funding supports the development of therapies for rare diseases, autoimmune disorders, and oncology. This investment highlights the potential of gene writing technology in precision medicine. Asceneuron Secures $100 Million for Tau Therapies Securing $100 million in Series C funding , Asceneuron SA will develop tau-targeting therapies for neurodegenerative diseases. The funding will support phase 2 clinical trials for Alzheimer’s disease, focusing on reducing tau protein tangles in the brain. Asceneuron aims to modify disease progression and improve cognitive function. Synchron Enhances BCI System with Generative AI US-based Synchron claims that it has integrated OpenAI's ChatGPT into its brain-computer interface (BCI) system, enhancing communication for non-verbal individuals with conditions like ALS. Coming on the back of several small BCI companies making similar claims, the credibility of which remains unsubstantiated in clinical setting. This integration would allow users to generate text and audio using their thoughts, improving communication speed and efficiency. The technology promises to significantly enhance the quality of life for patients with severe communication impairments. That being said, there are some really cool AI-BCI studies that have been reproduced, such as the Nishimoto Lab's work. AusperBio Raises $37 Million for HBV Cure Development The infection disease therapeutics startup, AusperBio, secured $37 million in Series A funding to advance AHB-137, its unconjugated antisense oligonucleotide therapy for hepatitis B. The drug originated from the company’s med-oligo technology suit, which allows them to design oligonucleotide-based treatments targeting viral RNA with unprecedented precision and efficacy in treating chronic infections. Cardurion Pharmaceuticals Raises $260 Million for Heart Disease The innovative cardiovascular disease company, Cardurion Pharmaceuticals, has raised $260 million in a Series B funding . The funding supports phase 2 clinical trials for heart failure treatments. Cardurion’s innovative therapies target the underlying mechanisms of heart disease to improve patient outcomes. Brainomix and Boehringer Ingelheim Enhance Lung Disease Care Boehringer Ingelheim has partnered with Brainomix to improve care for fibrosing lung disease using Brainomix’s FDA-cleared e-Lung imaging software. The collaboration aims to shorten diagnosis times and expedite treatment initiation. This partnership seeks to enhance patient outcomes in fibrosing lung diseases. CatalYm Secures $150 Million for Immunotherapy Pipeline Germany-based CatalYm received $150 million in Series D funding to advance its immunotherapy pipeline, focusing on a phase 2b clinical trial for solid tumors. The company’s lead candidate is an immune-modulating antibody designed to enhance the body's immune response against cancer. CatalYm aims to improve the efficacy of existing cancer immunotherapies. Draupnir Bio Raises €12 Million for Heart Disease Therapies Heart disease drug developer Draupnir Bio secured €12 million in seed funding to develop targeted protein degradation therapies for cardiovascular illnesses. The company’s preclinical research focuses on using small molecules to selectively degrade disease-causing proteins. Draupnir Bio aims to address the root causes of heart disease at the molecular level, rather than the mechanical level in which it is traditionally characterized and treated. Scorpion Therapeutics Announces $150 Million Series C Financing The boston-based precision oncology biotech, Scorpion Therapeutics has raised $150 million in Series C funding to support its precision oncology programs. This funding will advance phase 1/2 clinical trials for solid tumors, using a proprietary drug discovery platform to develop targeted therapies. Scorpion’s approach aims to deliver highly selective and potent treatments with fewer side effects. Sotio and Biocytogen Partner for $325M ADC Deal In a $325.5 million dea l, Sotio has partnered with Biocytogen to develop bispecific antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) for cancer treatment. The collaboration leverages Biocytogen's transgenic mice-generated bispecific antibodies to enhance Sotio's ADC capabilities. 🗓️ Upcoming BiB Events See our handy dandy Lu.ma event calendar HERE . title: Bits In Bio Weekly - July 16th 2024
text: 📢 Highlights BioOptimus comes out swinging with its foundation model: H-optimus-0 Flagship replenishes its fund with $3.6B for Boston Biotech investments Exscientia officially gets onboard the AI hype train with an AWS partnership Check out the IRL Bay Area meetups this month on the 24th and 25th ! Not yet a member of our super awesome slack community of >7000? Join HERE 🤗 👀 In Case You Missed it .. Bioptimus Introduces H-optimus-0 AI Model for Pathology French AI startup Bioptimus has launched H-optimus-0 , the largest open-source AI model for image-based pathology applications to date. Boasting 1.1 billion parameters, this advanced model has been trained on millions of histopathology images from 500,000 slides across 4000 clinical settings. This development was enabled by a $35M seed raise this past February from a diverse syndicate lead by European VC Sofinnova Ventures. To their credit, Bioptimus has chosen to opensource their model , enabling the global community of scientists and developers to develop novel digital pathology models and share the innovations they build on top of H-optimus-0. With an initial target of enhancing tissue-based cancer diagnostics, the model's performance reportedly aligns with or exceeds existing diagnostic tools. Biostate AI Launches RNA Sequencing and Data Analysis Services Emerging from stealth with $4 million in funding , Houston based Biostate AI is launching in earnest with the promise of providing a combo offering of Total RNA sequencing and an AI Copilot for RNAseq data analysis. By partnering with academia and biotech firms, they aim to enhance multiomic data collection and AI training with new diverse datasets. This service is designed to reduce costs and improve the acquisition and analysis of biological data, benefiting research and development in the biotech industry by providing a solution that is more in line with the emerging industry focus on tight design-make-test-learn cycles. Flagship Pioneering Raises $3.6B for Breakthrough Innovations With $3.6 billion secured, Flagship Pioneering plans to develop 25 companies focused on human health, sustainability, and AI. Supported by strategic partnerships with Pfizer, Samsung, and Thermo Fisher, this expansion increases Flagship's capital pool to $10.9 billion. The company also aims to foster global collaborations by establishing new hubs in London and Singapore, enhancing their ability to attract international talent and drive innovation on a global scale. In a recent interview, Flagship Pioneering’s legendary CEO, Noubar Afeyan, discusses the impact of their  newest fund on the biotech industry in a recent interview. Four key takeaways: Partnerships are a ‘bigger and bigger deal’ - Flagship is focusing on increasing and building on its current industry partnerships (such as with Pfizer , Samsung , Thermo Fisher and Novo Nordisk ). Afeyan recognises the increasing importance of such partners to help Flagship efficiently develop and grow its companies. What can’t AI do? Noubar believes in the potential of AI to disrupt biotech and is doubling down on its revolutionary potential through international collaborations and talent acquisition. Programmable medicines - e.g. RNA,  treatments that can be rapidly personalized for a given target, patient or scenario. doubling down on this focus with the launch of initiatives such as Pioneering Intelligence . Crush uncertainty - Flagship is famous for a unique approach to company creation, using “killer experiments” to weed out uncertainty. Exscientia Launches AWS-Powered AI Platform for Drug Discovery Exscientia has introduced an AWS-powered platform that combines generative AI with robotic lab automation to accelerate drug discovery. This platform supports the company's Design-Make-Test-Learn loops, aiming to reduce costs and enhance efficiency in drug development. By leveraging advanced AI/ML technologies, Exscientia hopes to streamline the drug discovery process, making it faster and more cost-effective. With the continuous roar of AI system claims from large organizations, the industry’s eyes will be on Exscientia as it demonstrates the merits of the new program. Eligo Bioscience Develops Gene Editing Tool for Gut Bacteria Targeting the emerging pharmacological domain of the gut microbiome, Eligo Bioscience has created a base editor tool that modifies gut bacteria genes in living mice without killing the bacteria, achieving over 90% target gene alteration in E. coli colonies. This tool, published in Nature, uses bacteriophage components for delivery and has successfully edited genes related to antibiotic resistance and neurodegenerative diseases. This innovation represents a significant advancement in the field of microbial gene editing, and hold promise for the diverse neurodegenerative and autoimmune diseases associated with the mammalian gut microbiome in ways that are still poorly understood and full of surprises, such as alpha-synuclein prior-like bodies coming up through the vasovagal nerve to induce parkinson’s disease. Komo Biosciences Launches with New Gene Insertion Technology The Honolulu based gene editing company, Komo Biosciences, has launched with integrase technology developed at the University of Hawaii, aiming to advance precision gene editing techniques. Supported by seed funding from Genesis BioCapital and others, the startup will operate in Honolulu and Cambridge, MA. This new technology is “driven by the power of highly-evolved and highly-efficient hyperactive integrases”, the molecular machinery originally responsible for the integration of viral DNA into hosts. Harnessing this potent mechanism is expected to improve gene insertion methods, potentially leading to significant advancements in genetic research and gene therapy. Iktos Acquires Synsight to Boost AI-Driven Drug Discovery The French/Russian chemoinformatics AI company, Synsight , has been acquired by Iktos  to enhance its AI-driven drug discovery platform with Synsight's MT Bench™ technology for protein-protein and RNA-protein interaction screening. This acquisition aims to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of drug discovery processes, leveraging AI to identify and optimize potential drug candidates more quickly and accurately. Aspect Biosystems Secures $72.75M for Bioprinting Technology Canadian biotech company Aspect Biosystems has secured $72.75 million from the Canadian and British Columbia governments to advance its bioprinting technology. This funding supports a $200 million multi-year project aimed at developing implantable tissue therapeutics. Aspect Biosystems' innovative bioprinting technology could revolutionize the creation of tissue therapies for a variety of medical applications. Kymera Therapeutics Expands Phase 2 Trials for KT-474 The Boston-based Targeted Protein Degrader (TPD) company, Kymera Therapeutics announced that Sanofi will expand Phase 2 trials of KT-474 (SAR444656) for Hidradenitis Suppurativa (HS) and Atopic Dermatitis (AD).  KT-474 is a first-in-class IRAK4 degrader, and its efficacy is being closely watched as a potential bellwether for PRD in kinase related indications. This decision follows a positive interim review of safety and efficacy data, accelerating progress towards pivotal studies. The expansion aims to further evaluate the drug's efficacy, with the goal of advancing to a phase 3 clinical trial. Biovance Launches with €51M to Boost Euro Biotech Alongside Venga Ventures, Biovance has launched with a €51 million fund to support biotech startups in Austria and Portugal. Their goal is to strengthen local biotech ecosystems and address gaps in translational work, providing crucial funding and support for early-stage drug development. This initiative is expected to boost innovation and growth in the Southern European biotech sector, which has long been considered stagnant due to a combination of cultural risk-aversion and a dearth of LP’s as compared to Boston and Bay area investment communities. Illumina Acquires Fluent BioSciences for Single-Cell Tech Single cell sequencing innovator Fluent BioSciences has been acquired by Illumina to enhance its single-cell analysis capabilities, integrating Fluent's PIPseq™ V technology into its product portfolio. Standing for ‘Pre-Templated Instant Partitions’, PIP’s function to simultaneously segregate complex cell mixtures with barcodes, enabling diverse single cell workflows. This acquisition aims to advance Illumina's multiomics growth strategy by making single-cell analysis technology more accessible to researchers. While dealing with the complexities of the Grail divestiture, Illumina continues to expand its capabilities in cutting-edge genomic analysis. ImmuneAge Emerges to Rejuvenate the Immune System The Swiss-US longevity company ImmuneAge has launched with $2 million in funding to rejuvenate the immune system through its ‘Immune Refresh’ approach, which focuses on expanding and rejuvenating bone marrow stem cells. The Swiss-US company, backed by notable investors, also identified a promising immune-enhancing drug. ImmuneAge's innovative approach aims to improve immune health and address age-related decline in immune function. The lean funding round is considered by some as potentially indicative of the EU investment industries continuing decline. Phesi's Study Shows Potential of AI-Powered Digital Twins The little known Connecticut-based clinical trial optimization firm, Phesi has shown in a recent study that it’s AI powered Bone Marrow Transplantation digital twins can replace standard of care control arms in clinical trials . This approach has the potential to streamline trial processes and reduce patient burden, particularly in conditions like chronic graft versus host disease (cGvHD). The study highlights the efficiency and accuracy of using AI in clinical research to improve trial outcomes, and the emergence of digital twins of biological systems. Parallel Bio to Make "Lights-Off" Lab for Drug Discovery Automation Let by tech veteran Ari Gesher, Parallel Bio is allegedly developing a fully automated "lights-off" lab to revolutionize drug discovery. Utilizing proprietary immune data and AI, the lab aims to improve pharma's R&D efficiency with organoid-based models for high-fidelity human testing. This innovative approach could significantly reduce the industry's high failure rate and accelerate the development of new therapies, provided it can avoid the pitfalls that have mired organizations like Emerald Cloud Labs, Strateos, and other challenging remote research solutions that launched with a similar set of optimistic promises only to encounter the constant adaptation to variance that human laboratory scientists perform subconsciously (sometimes without success). 🗓️ Upcoming BiB Events See our handy dandy Lu.ma event calendar HERE , please RSVP so folks can plan accordingly! title: Bits In Bio Weekly - July 9th 2024
text: 📢 Highlights Join us Thursday July 11th for the 1st ever Bits in Bio Global Learning Mixer ! Recursion is hot on Tempus’ heels, announcing a $200M public stock offering Bio AI darling, Owkin, expands into the DACH region with new programs The first-ever single dos gene therapy, HEMGENIX, launches in the EU Not yet a member of our super awesome slack community of >7000? Join HERE 🤗 👀 In Case You Missed it .. Recursion Pharmaceuticals Announces $200M Class A Stock Offering The Utah-based AI drug design company, Recursion Pharmaceuticals, has announced a $200 million public offering of Class A common stock, priced at $6.50 per share. Led by Goldman Sachs and J.P. Morgan, the offering includes an option for underwriters to purchase an additional 4.6 million shares, valued at around $30 million. Recursion went public in 2021 with an oversubscribed $306M IPO. This fresh round of funding will support Recursion's innovative approach to drug discovery characterized by high throughput morphological analysis with deep integration of data-native infrastructure and AI capabilities. This announcement comes on the heels of the $410M IPO of Tempus , completed only weeks prior. FDA Greenlights Eli Lilly’s Donanemab for Alzheimer’s Eli Lilly's Donanemab has received FDA approval for treating Alzheimer’s disease, offering new hope to patients. Clinical trials have shown that Donanemab slows cognitive decline in individuals with early symptomatic Alzheimer’s by targeting a modified form of beta amyloid called N3pG. Originally granted a ‘ Breakthrough Therapy’ designation in 2021 , the drug has weathered a storm of criticism around its modest efficacy and behavioral endpoints. Nonetheless, its approval marks a significant milestone for Eli Lilly and the Alzheimer's treatment landscape. Lilly intends to submit a biologics license application (BLA) for Donanemab under the accelerated approval pathway later this year based on data from its origins clinical trial, TRAILBLAZER-ALZ . Owkin Expands European Bio AI Research Collaborations The French AI-biotech unicorn Owkin is expanding into the DACH region (Germany, Austria, and Switzerland) partnering with nine hospitals to accelerate research in prostate cancer, muscle invasive bladder cancer (MIBC), and cardiovascular disease (CVD). By utilizing multimodal patient data and AI tool suite, Owkin aims to improve patient outcomes and develop personalized treatments with greater speed and safety than the status quo. The expansion will enhance collaborative research efforts while adding genetic diversity to Owkin’s datasets. Four Initial projects have been announced: BiomarkerPlus (Outcome prediction), TargetMatch (Target discovery), TrialPlus (Clinical trial optimization), AI Diagnostics ( MSIntuit® CRC ). Granza Bio's Seed Funding for Cancer Treatment Platform The San Francisco-based biotech startup Granza Bio has announced a successful seed funding round, raising $7.14 million . Led by Felicis and Refactor Capital, with participation from Y Combinator, this funding will advance Granza Bio's therapeutic delivery platform for cancer treatment. The platform leverages the discovery of the immune system’s suite of weapons called attack particles using them to enhance drug delivery efficiency and targeting specificity. The raised capital will support the development and clinical testing of new cancer treatments, but has not commented on the initial indications it will be focusing on. Groundbreaking Gene Therapy, HEMGENIX, Launched in EU CSL Behring, one of the global leaders in plasma derived therapeutics, has treated its first two patients in Europe with its Haemophilia B gene therapy drug HEMGENIX ® (etranacogene dezaparvovec). This treatment is the world's first FDA approved single dose gene therapy, and aims to deliver a functional copy of the F9 gene to the liver, potentially reducing or eliminating the need for regular infusions of clotting factor IX. The European launch follows FDA approval in late 2022, broadening patient access. The therapy has shown promising early results, with patients experiencing fewer bleeding episodes and improved quality of life. FDA Approves Signet Therapeutics’ AI-aided IND Cancer Drug Signet Therapeutics has received FDA approval for its Investigational New Drug (IND) application for sigx1094, designed utilizing a combination of AI and organoid disease models. This approval paves the way for Phase I clinical trials targeting diffuse gastric cancer and other advanced solid tumors. The Phase I trials will focus on evaluating the safety and efficacy of sigx1094 in human patients, with initial results expected to provide critical insights into its therapeutic potential. Waypoint Bio Raises $14.5M for CAR-T Therapy An innovative addition to CAR-T therapy development methods have secured Waypoint Bio $14.5 million in seed funding . Spun out of MIT by two co-founders, this new take on in-vivo spatial analysis technology enables rapid, parallel testing of numerous cell therapies in mice. Their method uses detailed molecular maps to identify where cell therapies encounter obstacles in attacking tumors, enabling therapeutic designers to optimize effective designs. Waypoint’s approach combines spatial biology with pooled screening to analyze cell therapy interactions within the tumor microenvironment at the single-cell level. The funding round, led by Hummingbird Ventures, will help expedite cell therapy development, aiming to select a lead cell therapy by the end of 2025. GSK Acquires CureVac's mRNA Vaccine Portfolio In a strategic move consistent with it’s ongoing M&A campaign, the British vaccine powerhouse GSK has invested $430 million upfront to acquire CureVac's (NASDAQ:CVAC) COVID-19 and flu mRNA vaccine portfolio , with undisclosed pending milestone payments. This deal aims to strengthen GSK’s capabilities in mRNA technology, which proved pivotal during the pandemic. The agreement includes potential milestone payments and is expected to enhance GSK’s vaccine pipeline significantly. The vaccines from CureVac are anticipated to complement GSK’s existing research prodigious deployment base that delivers over two million vaccine doses per day to people living in over 160 countries. Alumis Nets $250M Despite Original IPO Size Reduction Despite reducing its IPO size, Alumis has successfully raised $250 million to support its work in autoimmune diseases. The biotech company focus is on on developing  a TYK2 (a tyrosine kinase) inhibitor for plaque psoriasis. This substantial funding injection will advance Alumis' therapeutic pipeline, accelerating research and development activities.Alumis claims its drug, called ESK-001, could be more potent and selective than a recently approved TYK2 drug from Bristol Myers Squibb, called Sotyktu , though it has not yet proven itself in testing. Beacon Therapeutics Closes $170M Series B for Eye Gene Therapies Led by Forbion, Beacon Therapeutics has closed a $170 million Series B , The funds will advance the development of ophthalmic gene therapies for rare eye diseases using Beacon's innovative gene therapy platform. This investment will accelerate clinical trials and aim to bring novel gene therapies to patients suffering from rare ocular conditions. Astra Zeneca and Aptamer to Collaborate over Optimer-enabled siRNA Delivery Aptamer Group and AstraZeneca have joined forces to evaluate Optimer binders for targeted siRNA delivery to fibrotic liver cells. This collaboration aims to generate demonstrator data in animal models, enhancing non-viral delivery systems for gene therapies. The partnership seeks to advance the precision and efficacy of siRNA-based treatments. Successful demonstration in animal models could lead to human clinical trials, potentially revolutionizing gene therapy approaches for liver fibrosis. Paper: Revolutionizing Chemistry with LLMs and Autonomous Agents A comprehensive review of the transformative potential of large language models (LLMs) and autonomous agents in chemistry by the White lab of the University of Rochester highlights the near term capabilities of AI in biotech and pharmaceutical development. These technologies automate complex tasks, accelerating scientific discovery by predicting molecular properties and designing new molecules swiftly. High-quality datasets like ZINC, PubChem, and ChEMBL are essential for training these models, despite challenges in data quality and ethical considerations. Integrating LLMs into autonomous agents streamlines routine lab tasks, reducing human error and freeing researchers for strategic work. On of the major takeaways from this review is the need for improved benchmarks grounded in practical chemistry problems to fully realize the potential of LLMs in chemical applications. 🗓️ Upcoming BiB Events See our handy dandy Lu.ma event calendar HERE , please RSVP so folks can plan accordingly! title: Bits In Bio Weekly - June 25th 2024
text: 📢 Highlights EvolutionaryScale releases with ESM3 only weeks  after Isomorphic’s AF3 BioNTech and MediLink’s Lung Cancer Clinical Trial Paused After 3 deaths Insilico Medicine completes enrollment for its Chinese Phase IIa Study Join us for the SF BiB Tech Showcase this Thursday the 27th, with Atria and 8VC Not yet a member of our super awesome slack community of >7000? Join HERE 🤗 👀 In Case You Missed it .. Ex-Meta Protein AI Team Releases ESM3 and Nets a $142M Round of Funding With deep support from both Nvidia and AWS , the team behind Meta’s acclaimed protein folding tool ESM Fold have followed up their $40M pre-seed round with a second round of $142M. Following the surprising inclusion of  the bulk of the team in the massive layoffs in August 2023 , the all star squad of 8 was marshaled by the former head of the ESM project at Meta, Alex Rives. The company claims that its main product, ESM3 provides >50x increased speed over state of the art, amongst myriad other features. Their transformer-based approach has produced a frontier generative model for biology, “able to jointly reason across three fundamental biological properties of proteins: sequence, structure, and function”. In stark contrast to AlphaFold3, EvolutionaryScale has already provided an open version of their model on their GitHub . This is consistent with Meta’s historical dedication to open source AI tools, which has singularly been  reviving the tech giant’s brand, mired in a contentious history of alleged misconduct. The company has indicated its seed round will fund it for 2 years, indicating a weekly burn of around $1.5M, with $16 million going to computing power in the first year alone. This may sound like a large number, but makes sense given in the company's pitch deck states it plans to release a new model every year. The Bio-AI Community Has Tried AlphaFold3 … and Has Strong Opinions Since its unveiling only weeks ago, DeepMind and Isomorphic Lab’s AlphaFold3 has inspired deep analysis and discussion across the global Bio-AI community - Simon Barnett wrote up a comprehensive (and delightfully opinionated) field guide on AlphaFold3, reviewing its new features and improvements. The Boston Protein Design Club (BPDC), led by Chris Bahl of AI Proteins, did a walkthrough. You can see a video of it here. Carlos Outeiral looks at integrating AF3 into existing workflows with focus on its advancements around complex protein structures. Eva Smorodina examined the evolution of AlphaFold2 to AlphaFold3, including AlphaFold Multimer. Finally, while the absence of open source code and the limitations of the usage server make it tough to replicate or verify AF3's claims, the Open Source community has been hard at work making the first Pytorch implementations , with more to come. To their credit, DeepMind and Isomorphic labs have assured they will open source the model in around 5 months. Merck KGaA Partners with Biolojic Design for $376M Antibody Discovery The Germany based pharmaceutical giant, Merck KGaA has partnered with Biolojic Design to develop multi-specific antibodies using Biologic’s artificial intelligence tools and data. This collaboration, valued at $376 million, aims to create antibodies that can simultaneously target multiple disease mechanisms. By integrating Biolojic’s AI platform, the partnership seeks to enhance the precision and effectiveness of antibody therapies, potentially leading to significant medical breakthroughs in treating complex diseases such as cancer and autoimmune disorders AI-Designed Oncology Drug Receives Patent for Oncology Drug in Japan Lantern Pharma has been granted a composition of matter patent in Japan for its AI-designed oncology drug candidate, LP-284 . Currently undergoing Phase 1 trials for non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma and specific sarcomas, this patent strengthens Lantern Pharma’s intellectual property portfolio and supports the global development of LP-284. The drug is designed to target unique molecular pathways involved in cancer progression, offering a promising new treatment option that could improve patient outcomes through a precision medicine approach. FDA Pauses BioNTech/MedLink Trial Amid Safety Concerns A partial hold has been imposed by the FDA on the Phase I trial of the BioNTech/MedLink antibody-drug conjugate BNT326/YL202 following the deaths of three patients. This multi-center study sponsored by Suzhou based MedLink Therapeutics ( NCT05653752 ) was designed to assess BNT326/YL202 as a later-line treatment in heavily pre-treated patients with advanced or metastatic epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR)-mutated non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) or HR+/HER2-negative breast cancer. The study will continue with current patients while new U.S. enrollments are paused. The focus now shifts to exploring safer dosing regimens to mitigate observed risks. BioNTech and MedLink are working closely with the FDA to address these safety concerns and ensure that the trial can proceed under improved conditions. Insilico Completes Enrollment for Phase IIa Study, Moves to Cambridge, Mass. Participant enrollment has been completed by Insilico Medicine for its Chinese Phase IIa trial in China , targeting idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis with its compound INS018_055. The trial ( NCT05938920 ) expected to generate a comprehensive dataset by late Q4 2024, providing readouts on the drug’s efficacy and safety. The nature of the clinical trial has drawn broad skepticism around the iconoclastic company. With promising early results, Insilico plans to proceed with a Phase IIb proof-of-concept study in 2025. This progression highlights the potential of INS018_055 as a novel therapeutic option for patients suffering from this debilitating condition. In connection with this milestone, Insilico has announced that it will relocate its operational HQ’s from Hong Kong to Cambridge, Massachusetts. Enveda Biosciences Secures $55M in Series B2 Round In a recent funding round, Enveda Biosciences has raised $55 million , bringing its total funding to $230 million. This round included investments from notable entities such as Premji Invest, Microsoft, and Kinnevik. The capital infusion is reported to enable Enveda to accelerate its drug discovery efforts and expand its therapeutic pipeline. The company’s proprietary platform uses computational methods to identify and develop novel compounds from plants and other often overlooked phytological sources. Iambic Therapeutics Closes $50M for GNN Driven AI Oncology Drugs NeuralPLexer, an AI-driven platform from Iambic Therapeutics, has demonstrated superior performance in predicting protein-ligand structures, outperforming established systems like AlphaFold2. This achievement was featured in Nature Machine Intelligence . Alongside their ProPANE platform, which utilizes graph neural networks to optimize multiple drug properties, Iambic announced a significant funding boost with a $50 million round led by Mubadala Capital and Exor Ventures. This funding will support the advancement of their lead program IAM1363, targeting HER2-positive cancers, and the upcoming IAM-C1 program for solid tumors. SR One Secures Additional $200M for Biotech Investments With an additional $200 million raised for its growth opportunities fund, SR One doubles down on biotech following a successful $600 million fund closed in March 2023. This latest funding will support innovative biotech startups developing breakthrough therapies and technologies. SR One, formerly the corporate venture arm of GSK, focuses on companies at various stages of development, providing financial support and strategic guidance to drive innovation and advancement in the life sciences sector, ultimately aiming to improve patient outcomes. Roche Clears FDA for Digital Pathology System and Does $1.8B deal with Ascidian The Basel-based powerhouse Roche has received FDA 501(k) clearance to its Digital Pathology Dx system . This integrated system, featuring the VENTANA DP 200 slide scanner and workflow software, promises to accelerate pathologist diagnostic review and interpretation of patient histopathology slides. This integrated offering stands in stark contrast from the model-only innovations in routine diagnostic ML/AI, such a Microsoft’s recently unveiled GigaPath model , which should result in accelerated adoption amongst the notoriously conservative pathologist community. Additionally, Roche has entered into a strategic partnership with Ascidian Therapeutics to develop RNA exon editing therapies. This collaboration includes an initial investment of $42 million and potential milestone payments totaling up to $1.8 billion, aimed at advancing new treatments for neurological diseases. Kid-Sized Cures: Pediatric Access to Advanced Therapeutics A multi-institution perspective piece in Nature Medicine speaks to the challenges in making cell and gene therapies (CGTs) accessible for pediatric patients. Despite the transformative potential of CGTs in childhood diseases, their development has been hindered by high costs, small market sizes, complex regulatory landscapes, and the biological complexities of modifying the genes of a pre-pubescent human. To overcome these barriers, the authors propose the creation of the Pediatric Advanced Medicines Biotech (PAMB), which the authors describe as “an entity that would conduct registrational trials, sponsor biological license applications and commercialize approved CGTs for children”. The PAMB would leverage academic resources for manufacturing, collaborate closely with regulatory bodies, and implement new licensing practices tailored for pediatric needs. This approach aims to reduce manufacturing costs and expedite the approval process, getting more life-saving therapies to the children who need them. Examples of successful CGTs like Tisagenlecleucel ( Kymriah ) and Onasemnogene abeparvovec-xioi ( Zolgensma ) highlight the potential impact of these therapies. The authors, hailing from prestigious institutions such as Stanford University, Children’s National Hospital, and Baylor College of Medicine, bring a wealth of expertise and clout that may help the PAMB become a reality. 🗓️ Upcoming BiB Events See our handy dandy Lu.ma event calendar HERE , please RSVP so folks can plan accordingly! title: Bits In Bio Weekly - June 18th 2024
text: 📢 Highlights DeepMind releases Tx-LLM - a LLM for whole drug program development JP Morgan launches a half billion dollar health tech fund - their first ever GSK cleans up, with acquisition of Elsie and data licensure from Ochre We are starting our Interview Q&A Series - please weigh in on topics ! Not yet a member of our super awesome slack community of >6000? Join HERE 🤗 ✨ SPECIAL ANNOUNCEMENT ✨ - We’re Launching our Interview Q&A Series! Featuring new visionaries and proven innovators, we’ll explore trends, research, and developments, with guests sharing their career journeys and current projects. Each interview will be followed by an interactive Q&A on Slack so our community can engage directly. Stay tuned for updates, and let us know people and subjects you’d like to see covered. 👀 In Case You Missed it .. DeepMind Moves on Bio AI with Tx-LLM, an LLM for Therapeutic Development DeepMind's latest paper introduces Tx-LLM , a large language model fine-tuned from PaLM-2, tailored for the therapeutic development pipeline. Trained on 709 datasets spanning 66 tasks, Tx-LLM can handle various entities like small molecules, proteins, nucleic acids, and cell lines, combined with free-text instructions. This model predicts a broad range of properties and demonstrates competitive or superior performance compared to state-of-the-art models across many tasks, creating hope for a unified approach to drug discovery and clinical trials. Bridging Language Models and Reinforcement Learning in Drug Design with BindGPT The BindGPT framework has been introduced for 3D molecular design, combining language modeling and reinforcement learning has been released. This model generates molecular graphs and conformations jointly within a protein's binding site, bypassing the need for extra graph reconstruction - a laborious and often expensive process. Pretrained on extensive datasets and fine-tuned with reinforcement learning, the same paradigm that gave rise to OpenAi’s suite of chatbots, BindGPT serves multiple roles: as a 3D molecular generative model, a conformer generator, and a pocket-conditioned molecule generator. It demonstrates performance on par or better than specialized models, while being 100x cheaper. PathChat: A Multimodal AI for Human Pathology Developed by a multidisciplinary team at Harvard University and recently described in Nature, the PathChat AI assistant combines a vision encoder with a large language mode fine-tuned on 456,000 diverse visual language instructions consisting of 999,202 question-answer turns. This multimodal model assists in diagnostic tasks by providing pathologist-preferable responses, outperforming other text-only AI assistants with its ability to handle both visual and character inputs. PathChat's versatility is its main selling point - with use cases in pathology education, research, and clinical decision-making. Demonstrating equivalent or superior performance in accuracy, consistency, and integration of complex diagnostic information to SOTA, PathChat is slated for pilot deployment in Massachusetts based medical research centers. Nemotron-4 by NVIDIA Optimizes LLM Training NVIDIA has unveiled Nemotron-4 340B, an advanced synthetic data generation pipeline designed to enhance LLM training. Optimized for NVIDIA NeMo and TensorRT-LLM, Nemotron-4 includes instruct and reward models and a diverse dataset to improve AI applications like natural language processing and autonomous systems. Data scarcity is one of the major issues holding back new AI feature development, a problem synthetic datasets provide a solution to. However, their use has been drought, due to both quality and consistency issues. By generating high-quality, varied data, Nemotron-4 aims to significantly increase the efficiency and performance of both Nvidia and its customers LLMs, enabling more accurate and robust AI solutions for use cases limited by data sparsity. Santa Ana Bio Secures $168M Series A for Autoimmune Disease Therapies Santa Ana Bio has successfully raised $168 million in Series A and B funding to develop precision therapies for autoimmune and inflammatory diseases. Leveraging advanced proteomic, transcriptomic, and genomic techniques, the company aims to identify specific biomarkers and disease pathways. This targeted approach promises more effective and personalized treatments, potentially transforming the management of autoimmune and inflammatory diseases by providing therapies that address the underlying causes of these conditions. J.P. Morgan’s $500M Biotech Venture Capital Fund J.P. Morgan Life Sciences Private Capital has closed its inaugural biotechnology venture capital fund with over $500 million in commitments. This fund is focused on investing in innovative biotechnology companies at various stages of development. Emphasizing novel therapeutic modalities, advanced diagnostics, and cutting-edge technologies, the fund aims to drive significant advancements in the life sciences sector. Hoth Therapeutics and Wise Systems Leverage NVIDIA AI for Drug Discovery In collaboration with Wise Systems International, Hoth Therapeutics will u tilize Nvidia's BioNeMo AI platform to enhance their drug discovery efforts. This partnership aims to streamline Hoth’s research pipeline from target identification to lead optimization by integrating generative AI. Nvidia's advanced AI capabilities will help improve the precision and efficiency of Hoth's drug discovery processes, potentially accelerating the development of novel therapeutics for a variety of diseases and bringing new treatments to market faster. Foresite Capital’s $900M Fund for Precision Medicine The recent $900 million fund raised by Foresite Capital is dedicated to supporting startups in healthcare and life sciences. With a focus on precision therapeutics, life science infrastructure, and healthcare delivery, this fund aims to accelerate personalized medicine advancements. Notable companies in their portfolio, such as 10x Genomics and Relay Therapeutics, highlight the confidence investors have in precision healthcare. Ochre Bio and GSK’s Data License Agreement For Liver Disease Ochre Bio has entered into a multi-year data license agreement with GS K, valued at a comparatively modest $37.5 million. This partnership will grant GSK access to Ochre Bio’s proprietary platforms to generate bespoke datasets for liver disease research. By leveraging Ochre Bio’s advanced data analytics capabilities, GSK aims to gain deeper insights into the mechanisms driving liver diseases. This collaboration could lead to the discovery of novel therapeutic targets and the development of more effective treatments for liver-related conditions, a historically challenging area of therapeutic development. GSK Expands RNA Pipeline with Elsie Acquisition With the acquisition of Elsie for $50 million , GSK is set to expand its growing RNA therapeutics pipeline. Elsie specializes in developing RNA molecules that modulate gene expression, offering potential treatments for a wide range of diseases. This acquisition aligns with GSK’s strategy to leverage innovative RNA technologies and accelerate the development of new therapies. Elsie’s expertise will complement GSK’s existing capabilities, potentially leading to the creation of novel RNA-based treatments that address unmet medical needs - Elsie’s participation in which has not been disclosed. 🗓️ Upcoming BiB Events See our handy dandy Lu.ma event calendar HERE , please RSVP so folks can plan accordingly! title: Bits In Bio Weekly - June 11th 2024
text: 📢 Highlights Our community has grown past 7000 members! 23&me’s little known  therapeutics arm has had a positive initial clinical trial read The much anticipated Seattle happy hour is happening this week, don’t miss out! NIH scientist use log regression for effective prediction of immunotherapy response, showing you don’t need neural nets for everything. We’re starting an Interview Q&A Series - give us topics/people suggestions here ! Not yet a member of our super awesome slack community of >7000? Join HERE 🤗 👀 In Case You Missed it .. Therapeutic Arm of 23&me Scores Needed Win with its Anti-CD200R1 Antibody 23andMe Therapeutics, the biotech spinout from the eponymous consumer genetic testing company, reported positive preliminary Phase 2 data for its puckishly titled investigational drug candidate 23ME-00610, a first-in-class anti-CD200R1 antibody, from the ongoing Phase 1/2a clinical trial (NCT05199272) evaluating across several solid tumor types with expansion cohorts in clear cell renal cell carcinoma, ovarian/peritoneal cancer, neuroendocrine tumors, and cancers with high mutation burden.Notably, a pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor patient experienced a confirmed partial response lasting over 21 months, while an ovarian cancer patient exhibited durable benefit exceeding 12 cycles. 23andMe used its database of over 12 million genotyped customers to identify genetic variants linked to CD200/CD200R1 signaling and cancer risk, enabling the development of 23ME-00610. Though preliminary, a positive result of the ongoing clinical trial could provide validation for the recently embattled public company. Optogenetics Gets Ready for Prime Time - Prolific Machines Closes $55M series B. Prolific Machines, a California-based startup harnessing light-sensitive proteins for biomanufacturing, secured $55 million in a Series B1 led by Fonterra's Ki Tua Fund to advance its optogenetics platform that enables unprecedented cellular control across industries like vaccines, therapeutics, and food ingredients. The ‘optogenetics’ technology may sound familiar to those in neuroscience - it was originally developed as a research tool for studying neuronal signaling and is the life long project of its creator, Karl Diesseroth . The funding round, which brings Prolific Machines' total raised to $86.5 million, was also supported by investors like Breakthrough Energy Ventures, Mayfield, SOSV, and others. Prolific Machines introduces light-sensitive proteins into cells used for biomanufacturing, then uses light to activate or deactivate specific genes, proteins, or cellular pathways, providing a level of precision not possible with traditional methods that rely on adding expensive molecules to cell culture media Logistic Regression to Predict Patient Response to Immune Checkpoint Blockade Authors from NIH and Memorial Sloan Kettering released a surprisingly effective logistic regression approach for predicting Checkpoint inhibitor patient outcomes, disclosed in their recent paper and made available online for general use through the National Cancer Institute. Using historical data about patient response to immune checkpoint inhibitors (either CTLA-4 or PD-L1 ), their model attempts use features that are fairly easy to measure such as “treatment type” or “age”. Only 20 such features were present in the data, and 8 present in all samples. Using these data, the authors threw pretty much all the functions and methods in sklearn to the classification of their tabular data. They notably left out more contemporary data that is harder for most labs to get their hands on, such as DNA and RNA sequencing reads. In the end they claim that the best performing model is a good old Lasso on 6 features. The model generalizes well within distribution, which is not surprising since overfitting such a model requires very active intervention.Though promising in the simplicity of its approach and the quality of the predictions of its model, this paper likely needs to be taken with a grain of salt - it is being used only on data with a very small set of features, for which simple models are indeed expected to work well. The claim that genomics data is too expensive in cancer treatment can come across as a bit misdirected, since RNA-seq sequencing experiment costs $400, and ICB therapy costs at least $50k - and that is a very conservative estimate for the US healthcare system. ITM Isotope Technologies, a Leader in Radiotherapeutics, Lands >$200M inFunding Germany-based therapeutic developer ITM Isotope Technologies Munich secured a €188 million ($204.6 million) investment led by Temasek to advance its radiopharmaceutical pipeline, including its Phase III lead candidate ITM-11 for treating gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors. The asset is currently under evaluation in two Phase III trials, COMPETE and COMPOSE , for the treatment of gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumours.The funding will also bolster ITM's manufacturing capabilities for Lutetium-177 and expand production to include other medical radioisotopes like Actinium-225, diversifying its radiopharmaceutical therapy pipeline to address several additional cancer indications directly and with a global network of partners. Envisagenics Lands Series B to Map the Spliceosome for Nucleotide-Based Drugs New York based Envisagenics, an AI-driven biotech, has made significant advancements in RNA splicing therapeutics through its proprietary SpliceCore platform. The company has secured $25 million in Series B funding , including a strategic investment from Bristol Myers Squibb, to advance its preclinical oncology pipeline. RNA splicing, a crucial process that occurs in over 95% of human genes, involves removing non-coding introns from precursor mRNA and joining the remaining coding exons. Errors in splicing can lead to the production of aberrant proteins implicated in various diseases, including cancer and neurodegeneration. By uncovering disease-associated splicing patterns, SpliceCore can guide the design of RNA-based therapies, such as antisense oligonucleotides (ASOs), which can correct aberrant splicing by inducing exon skipping or inclusion. SpliceCore leverages machine learning algorithms and high-performance computing to analyze a database of >14 million RNA splicing events, far exceeding the scope of existing databases. By mapping this expansive "spliceomic" landscape, SpliceCore can allegedly pinpoint novel, disease-specific alternative splicing isoforms that could serve as therapeutic targets. Arch Venture Partners Lead $71M Round for Baker lab Macrocyclic Peptide Spinout Vilya, a biotech company founded by Arch Venture Partners that is pioneering a new class of macrocycle medicines, raised $71 million in an expanded Series A round from led by its originator but with the added participation of NVIDIA's NVentures, Menlo Ventures, Madrona, and other. Macrocyclic peptides hold huge promise, their ring-shaped structures can improve target binding, membrane permeability, and metabolic stability compared to linear molecules. However, designing effective macrocycles has been challenging using traditional methods. The financing will significantly enhance Vilya's proprietary generative AI platform, which was originally developed by the legendary Baker lab at the University of Washington, which aims to overcome the significant hurdles associated with macrocyclic peptide drug design. A recent study in Science validated Vilya's computational approach, which can explore vast chemical spaces beyond what traditional random screening methods can achieve. 🗓️ Upcoming BiB Events See our handy dandy Lu.ma event calendar HERE , please RSVP so folks can plan accordingly! title: Bits In Bio Weekly - June 4th 2024
text: 📢 Highlights The FDA issues new guidance on platform biotech companies’ approval processes England’s NHS boldly deploys cancer vaccines to thousands to sustain remission Hundreds of peer reviewed papers revealed to use non-existent cancer cell lines Bits in Bio “Bits in Bio Global Learning Mixer” inbound! Contribute here Not yet a member of our super awesome slack community of >6000? Join HERE 🤗 👀 In Case You Missed it .. AstraZeneca Adds Benevolent AI’s Heart Drug to its Portfolio In partnership since 2019, Benevolent AI and AstraZeneca have announced the first fruit of their multi-year collaboration , leveraging the former’s AI drug discovery platform and AstraZeneca’s prodigious experimental infrastructure. The drug will target heart failure, a pathophysiologically diverse malady currently approached with a one size fits all framework. The announcement is largely shrouded in mystery, though it has been disclosed that financial dimensions of both the initial and extended collaborations include upfront payments, research funding, potential discovery, development and commercial milestones. New FDA Guidance for Platform Technologies to Streamline Drug Approvals In what could prove to be a historic shift in regulatory guidance, the FDA is following through on its intimations of interest in creating regulatory frameworks for platform drug development technologies. Released only days ago, this unprecedented draft regulation describes what a platform technology is as based on its fulfillment of  the following three criteria: 1.) Is concerned with structurally similar drug substances, such as similarly sized nucleic acid sequences with comparable backbone chemistry, subunit modifications, and targeting moieties. 2.) There are minimal differences in drug product formulation, qualitatively and quantitatively; and/or… 3.) There are nearly identical manufacturing processes for drug substance and/or drug product manufacturing, and purification. The ramifications are manifold, and the totality of their impact will evolve over time as the industry responds. What we can say is that being a platform technology will allow clinical sponsors to leverage existing evidence from previous trials in their future approvals, as well as early advice from the FDA on how to go through their process, hopefully expediting a currently labyrinthine approval processes without increasing patient risk. This is very meaningful move by the FDA,  as it shows a willingness from the regulators to embrace the notion of “platform biotechs” (as Moderna gets its second vaccine approved, this time for RSV), and a material step forward in lowering the costs of clinical validation thereby reducing the trend of ballooning new therapeutic development costs. We can expect this to have an impact in the startup world too, as it will affect the economics of startups, and may allow them to seriously reduce their need to find  “big Pharma” sponsors to run their expensive trials. DeepCure Shows ‘Inspired Chemistry’, its Entry to AI-assisted Chemical Synthesis Manual synthesis of chemical libraries has historically been a limitation when exploring large chemical search spaces. DeepCure is seeking to change that with its high-throughput, multi-step r obotic synthesis platform called ‘Inspired Chemisty’ . With the vision of addressing the limitations of manual chemical synthesis and paving the way for AI-designed compound design-synthesize-test-repeat development loops, Inspired Chemistry provides promise for advancing the reach of AI powdered R&D. In a recent demonstration, the Inspired Chemistry platform synthesized Nirmatrelvir , a key component of Paxlovid, along with 56 analogs through its robot-driven workflow. Enveda reveals PRISM: a Microsoft-Powered AI Model for Interpreting Chemistry A newly-announced collaboration with Microsoft will grant Enveda Biosciences access to Microsoft’s cloud compute resource ecosystem, called Azure, to power PRISM (Pretrained Representations Informed by Spectral Masking) and the continued development of its other GenAI drug discovery tools. PRISM, designed to "read and translate" life's chemistry, is trained on the largest collection of mass spectra ever assembled— over one billion small molecular spectra. This represents a significant advancement in using AI to interpret mass spectrometry data for the prediction of structures and identification of unknown natural molecules. Historically an art as much a science, streamlining mass spec data interpretation could have broad utility. FinalSpark Introduces Neuroplatform for Human Brain Organoid Biocomputing The innovative Swiss biocomputing startup, FinalSpark, has unveiled Neuroplatform, an inspiring step forward in wetware computing and organoid bioprocessor technology. The innovative tool integrates hardware, software, and biology, leveraging 16 human brain organoids. Though not yet comparable to existing silicon based processors, the use of cellular constructs brings an extraordinary reduction in power consumption— requiring a million times less power than traditional chips . The most critical limitation to bioprocessor technologies is the life cycle of biological systems - these organoids have lifespans of approximately 100 days. Nevertheless, over the past three years, Neuroplatform has facilitated experiments with more than 1,000 brain organoids, generating 18 terabytes of data. Check out the details of the research here . This breakthrough in biocomputing not only highlights the potential of organoid intelligence but also opens new avenues for advancements in electrophysiology and artificial intelligence. Turn Biotechnologie and HanAll Advance mRNA Therapies for Diseases of Aging The California based preclinical mRNA therapeutics company, Turn Biotechnologies, and Korean biomanufacturer HanAll Biopharma have entered into an exclusive global licensing agreemen t to develop novel treatments for age-related eye and ear diseases. The agreement, presaged by HanAll’s initial investment in Turn in 2022, is valued at over $300 million, and grants HanAll the right to utilize Turn Bio's Epigenetic Reprogramming of Aging (ERA™) and eTurna™ delivery platform."The power of ERA™ technology is that it can rejuvenate cells in virtually any organ in the body," said Turn Bio Co-founder Vittorio Sebastiano, the company's head of research. "This agreement enables us to accelerate our development on multiple fronts to reverse the damage caused by aging and improve the quality of life for older patients." England’s National Health Service Pioneers Cancer Vaccine Deployment Study In a bold move motivated by the continued progress of the cancer vaccine therapeutics, thousands of patients in England's National Health Service (NHS) will receive personalized mRNA-based cancer vaccinations . Designed to prime the immune system to target and destroy remaining cancer cells, this treatment strategy aims to reduce recurrence risk, keeping patients cancer free. The Cancer Vaccine Launch Pad , a NHS program for connecting patients to vaccines, has recruited 30 hospitals for this initiative, with initial treatments described as “showing promise”. Hundreds of Cancer papers Found to be Using Cell Lines That Don’t Actually Exist In a recent multidisciplinary study led by the University of Sydney and puckishly titled “Misspellings or ‘miscellings’—Non-verifiable and unknown cell lines in cancer research publications” researchers report inconsistencies and nomenclature problems in cell line identifiers across hundreds of papers in prestigious cancer journals. While some of these might be a matter of misspellings and later lack of correction, the study nonetheless found >200 published cancer manuscripts with cell line information that does not exist and can’t be confirmed by any known standard, which most likely represents an uncovering inadequate research behavior. Though there is a valid argument that this phenomena is simply a series of oversights, the process of multiple editors providing review of each article, a la “Peer Review” would seem to erode the foundation of that perspective. This finding serves a red flag for fraudulent studies at least in cancer research,  highlighting a need for stricter validation measures, to ensure credibility and reliability of research reports. 🗓️ Upcoming BiB Events See our handy dandy Lu.ma event calendar HERE , please RSVP so folks can plan accordingly! title: Bits In Bio Weekly - May 28th 2024
text: 📢 Highlights Welcome back from the long weekend - the US took the time off yesterday for  Memorial Day, a remembrance of the great sacrifices made for the nation USP1 Inhibition as a drug target proves problematic across multiple programs New foundation models are being released weekly as AI permeates biotech Toronto’s Bits in Bio Happy Hour kicks off this evening! Don’t miss out. Not yet a member of our super awesome slack community of >6000? Join HERE 🤗 👀 In Case You Missed it .. Tempus AI Officially Files S-1 Registration Statement for an IPO The health technology company, Tempus, has filed for an IPO , aiming to raise up to $100 million. Founded in 2015, Tempus has been a leader in precision medicine and AI-driven diagnostics with its vast library of clinical and molecular data, boasting partnerships with major pharmaceutical companies and a rapidly expanding suite of AI-powered solutions. Tempus has pocketed $1.3 billion in funding over nine funding rounds. Two years ago, the tech company banked $200 million in financing rounds , reportedly driving its valuation to $8.1 billion. Don’t get too excited though, BloomBerg Reports that “Chicago-based Tempus AI had a net loss of $266 million on revenue of $532 million in 2023, compared with a net loss of $334 million on revenue of $321 million a year earlier, according to the filing”. Read the press release here USP1 Inhibition Struggle - Tango Halts TNG348, Roche KSQ-4279 found Ineffective Tango Therapeutics has discontinued its USP1 inhibitor TNG348 due to severe liver toxicity , while Roche’s KSQ-4279 shows minimal efficacy and concerning toxicity in early human trials. This casts doubt on the future of USP1 inhibitors, a mechanism recently touted for its potential in cancer treatment. With Exelixis and other companies heavily invested in similar projects, the unfolding results could reshape the landscape of DNA damage response therapies. Continued disappointing performance may limit the attractiveness of the target across the industry. LabGenius Fuels Antibody Breakthroughs with £35M Series B Funding Touting its Eva AI drug discovery system, LabGenius has raised £35 million in a Series B round led by M Ventures. A pioneer in ML-driven antibody discovery, the 12 year old London based company is planning to use Eva to help it develop a pipeline of novel multispecific antibodies for treating solid tumors. EVA purportedly uses advanced AI and robotic automation to design, conduct, and learn from experiments, increasing the efficiency of antibody optimization. LabGenius will also continue building and expanding its strategic partnerships, including its present collaboration with Sanofi. Microsoft Presents GigaPath Model for Whole Slide Histopathological Analysis GigaPath, a vision transformer based foundation model designed for gigapixel whole-slide images in digital pathology, was released by Microsoft. This model leverages dilated attention to efficiently process massive images, significantly improving cancer classification and pathomics tasks. In collaboration with Providence and the University of Washington, GigaPath’s large-scale pretraining on real-world data has set new benchmarks in precision health, promising enhanced diagnostics and treatment predictions for the mega-tech companies customers. In addition to the commercial release, Microsoft also is providing access to an open source version - prov-Gigapath . Insilico Medicine Releases Another AI model: nach0 Insilico Medicine, maker of hundreds of close sourced AI models and services for drug development, has announced nach0, a large language model tailored for chemical and biomedical tasks. Harnessing a vast dataset of 100 million documents and leveraging computer provided to them through their Nvidia partnership, nach0  integrates natural language  with molecular generation and synthetic route prediction. With superior performance in molecular tasks and potential applications in generating therapeutic options, nach0 represents a credible advancement in  novel therapeutic discovery automation. Read the related article here and the Chemical Science Journal publication here . Oxford Nanopore Incorporates Transformers to Boost Base-calling Accuracy Oxford Nanopore Technologies continued to deepen its use of AI systems in its products. Building on its succesfull partnership with Nvidia , the innovative sequencing company is integrating transformer models into their basecalling system. These transformers allow parallel processing of data, enhancing speed and efficiency compared to traditional LSTM models. Using sliding window multi-head attention and internal sequence compression, the new transformer basecaller model achieves higher accuracy with reduced computational costs. This advancement is set to accelerate research in areas like cancer genomics and personalized medicine, making large-scale genomic analysis more accessible and efficient. Adderall Shortage Relief: Teva Recovers Supply, Pharmacies Still Struggle The Adderall shortage crisis caused by the policy stumble of the United State’s Drug Enforcement Agencies (DEA’s) abrupt production limits and a rising trend in ADHD diagnoses, is begging to loosen. Israeli drug manufacturer Teva has managed to stabilize its supply , with all but the 5-mg generic version now available. The company warns that shortages will continue due to the companies limited capacity - as other manufacturers struggle with ingredient shortages, patients nationwide still face difficulties filling prescriptions. Meanwhile, legislative efforts are underway to address the broader drug shortage crisis Collaboration in AI Drug Development: Sanofi, Formation Bio, and OpenAI Sanofi continues to expand its AI footprint through collaborations with Formation Bio and OpenAI. The partnership plans to combine Sanofi’s  drug data, OpenAI’s AI stack, and Formation Bio’s expertise in tech-driven drug development to create AI solutions across the therapeutics development lifecycle. Insights into Alzheimers Pathophysiology Inspires Hope Alzheimers disease, traditionally intractable in both therapeutic development and basic research programs, a recent publication from a multi-institute research team has credibly identified the APOE4 deterministically induces an increased risk to develop the cognitive wasting disease. The analysis included 3,297 individuals for the pathological study and 10,039 for the clinical study. This is a meaningful result will allow targeted therapeutics and diagnostics - finally, the medical community can start to divide Alzheimers into different diseases (the same way that we divided breast cancer via HER/ER/PgR. Read the article here 🗓️ Upcoming BiB Events See our handy dandy Lu.ma event calendar HERE , please RSVP so folks can plan accordingly!
